commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b11e2c918f0bffb782f22da25c68487c0b044584)
commands.review_summary:
The PR gates the generic DeepSeek V4 OpenAI-compatible thinking wrapper on `model.compat.thinkingFormat`, adds focused payload mutation tests, and adds an unreleased changelog line.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +59, Docs +1. Total +76 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. there is a high-confidence source reproduction: current main matches DeepSeek V4 OpenAI-compatible ids without consulting `compat.thinkingFormat`, then the shared wrapper injects `thinking`. I did not run a live Azure Foundry call.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] External PR proof must come from the contributor or a maintainer-owned environment, and the small changelog cleanup can be handled during normal review.

target.title: fix(agents): respect compat.thinkingFormat override for DeepSeek V4 models
target.body:
## Summary

- Reasoning-enabled DeepSeek V4 models always received the deepseek-native `thinking: { type }` payload, even when the model config set `compat.thinkingFormat` to another format. OpenAI-compatible deployments such as Azure AI Foundry reject the `thinking` parameter outright (HTTP 400), so DeepSeek V4 was unusable through the gateway there. (#87737)
- `compat.thinkingFormat` is the documented escape hatch for non-standard reasoning wire formats, but it was silently ignored for DeepSeek V4 because the wrapper gated only on model id + api. That left affected users with no config-level workaround.
- Intended outcome: an explicit `compat.thinkingFormat` override takes priority over the id-based DeepSeek V4 auto-detection. When it selects a non-deepseek format (for example `"openai"`), the native `thinking` field is no longer injected.
- Intentionally out of scope: the MiMo reasoning wrapper and other providers are unchanged. Default DeepSeek V4 behavior (no override) is unchanged, and an explicit `thinkingFormat: "deepseek"` still gets the native payload.
- Reviewers: please confirm the gate runs at stream time on the resolved model's `compat` (mirrors the existing `createOpenAICompletionsStoreCompatWrapper` pattern) and only affects the DeepSeek V4 application, not the MiMo one directly below it.

## Linked context

Closes #87737

Related #87746 (catalog availability; distinct from this thinking-schema issue).

The issue is maintainer-triaged `clawsweeper:queueable-fix` + `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear` + `clawsweeper:source-repro`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: the DeepSeek V4 wrapper ignored `compat.thinkingFormat` and injected `thinking`, causing `400 Unrecognized request argument supplied: thinking` on Azure AI Foundry DeepSeek V4 deployments.
- Real environment tested: source-level reproduction in the actual wrapper-application path (`applyPostPluginStreamWrappers` in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.ts`), validated with red/green unit tests. I do **not** have an Azure AI Foundry DeepSeek V4 deployment, so I could not capture the live 400 round-trip.
- Exact command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (green): 4/4 pass.

  ```
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  4 passed (4)
  ```

- Evidence before fix (red — temporarily reverting just the new gate): the two suppression cases fail, proving the regression guard is real.

  ```
  × does not inject thinking when thinkingFormat is openai (Azure Foundry)
  × does not inject thinking:disabled when thinkingFormat is openai and thinking is off
  Tests  2 failed | 2 passed (4)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: with `compat.thinkingFormat: "openai"`, the outbound payload contains no `thinking` key (both reasoning-on and reasoning-off). Default (unset) and explicit `"deepseek"` keep `thinking: { type: "enabled" }` plus `reasoning_effort`.
- What was not tested: live Azure AI Foundry DeepSeek V4 round-trip (no access to such a deployment).
- Proof limitations: no Azure Foundry credentials; the issue is labeled `source-repro` and the fix is verified against the exact wrapper path the issue identifies. Happy to coordinate a live capture if a maintainer has access.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts` (new, 4 cases) — pass
- `pnpm test extra-params.zai-tool-stream.test.ts provider-stream-shared.test.ts embedded-agent-runner-extraparams.test.ts` — 146 pass, no regressions
- `pnpm tsgo` — 0 errors
- `oxlint` and `oxfmt --check` on touched files — clean

Regression coverage added: new test covering (1) default injects native thinking, (2) `thinkingFormat: "openai"` suppresses thinking with reasoning on, (3) suppresses `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` with reasoning off, (4) explicit `thinkingFormat: "deepseek"` keeps native thinking.

What failed before this fix: cases (2) and (3) — the unpatched wrapper injected `thinking` regardless of `thinkingFormat` (red output shown above).

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? **Yes** — DeepSeek V4 models with a non-deepseek `compat.thinkingFormat` override no longer receive a `thinking` field on reasoning runs.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? **No** — no new config; honors the existing `compat.thinkingFormat` contract.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? **No.**
- Highest-risk area: accidentally suppressing native thinking for genuine DeepSeek V4 endpoints.
- Mitigation: suppression triggers only on an **explicit** non-deepseek `thinkingFormat`. Unset keeps id-based auto-detection; explicit `"deepseek"` keeps the native payload. Both are covered by tests.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Still waiting on: optional live Azure Foundry proof (I lack access to that deployment).
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: none yet.
target.author: mukundakatta
